ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
fix(aws): pass default region to fetch regions (#12175)

Co-authored-by: Gauthier Delacroix <no@email.com>
removed_lines: -    aws ec2 describe-regions |grep RegionName | awk -F ':' '{gsub(/"/, "", $2);gsub(/,/, "", $2);gsub(/ /, "", $2);  print $2}'
added_lines: +    aws ec2 describe-regions --region $region |grep RegionName | awk -F ':' '{gsub(/"/, "", $2);gsub(/,/, "", $2);gsub(/ /, "", $2);  print $2}'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: fix(git): add checked-out branch support to `gbg*` (#12397)
removed_lines: -alias gbgd='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -d'
-alias gbgD='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -D'
added_lines: +alias gbgd='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | cut -c 3- | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -d'
+alias gbgD='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | cut -c 3- | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -D'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: fix(pip): fix `pipupall` error with `freeze` format (#11280)
removed_lines: -  pip list --outdated --format freeze | cut -d= -f1 | ${=xargs} pip install --upgrade
added_lines: +  pip list --outdated | awk 'NR > 2 { print $1 }' | ${=xargs} pip install --upgrade

Fixes #5653

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
Fixed `fwl` function in `firewalld` plugin when `sources` used (#7011)

`firewall-cmd --get-active-zones` returns something like this:

```
dmz
  sources: ipset:dmz-hosts
public
  interfaces: eth0
```

if zone binding is based on source ips, so strings with `sources: ...` should be excluded along with `interfaces: ...` to get zones list.
Update z to latest version (#7021)

Use existing ssh-agent when invoking a sudo shell (#3891)

When invoking a shell as root using ```sudo -s```, the ssh-agent plugin
starts a new agent although it already exists.

The problem boils down to a check if ssh-agent is running using
```ps x```. If that is extended to ```ps ax``` for root, then the
existing ssh-agent will still work.

rand-quote: update URL

Fixes #6949

Fix mix-fast plugin (#6708)

The mix command for listing all available actions has been changed from
`mix --help` to `mix help`.

Better app error handling in curl (#5828)

Deals with app error page, saving true error instead.

Upon app failure, Heroku returns HTML "Application Error" page.
Finding HTML page in .gitignore is confusing, so I replaced
`-s` with `-f` in curl calls, which cuts such output.

Replace instead of addition as no progress meter outputs either.

      -- E. W. Dijkstra

-        -Dit.test= `if [ -d ./src/test/java ] ; then find ./src/test/java -type f -name '*.java' | grep -v svn | sed 's?.*/\([^/]*\)\..*?-Dit.test=\1?' ; fi`
added_lines: +        -Dtest=$(if [ -d ./src/test/java ] ; then find ./src/test/java -type f -name '*.java' | grep -v svn | sed 's?.*/\([^/]*\)\..*?-Dtest=\1?' ; fi)
+        -Dit.test=$(if [ -d ./src/test/java ] ; then find ./src/test/java -type f -name '*.java' | grep -v svn | sed 's?.*/\([^/]*\)\..*?-Dit.test=\1?' ; fi)

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
ssh-agent: check ssh-agent process w/ ps again

The alternative is using tools that aren't available everywhere.
The latest report is that cygwin/msys2 doesn't have pgrep.

Fixes #5418.
Fix git_prompt_status() not showing ahead/behind/diverged status correctly (#5388)

Moved git.io url to https

git.io now redirects to https. So the current function break. Changing it to https fixes the issue.
Delete useless `/usr/bin/env` in ssh-agent
gulp plugin: missing opening double quote & had unneeded parens on function
removed_lines: -    compls=$(grep -Eo "gulp.task\((['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"],)" gulpfile.js 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo "['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"]" | sed s/"['\"]"//g | sort)"
added_lines: +    compls="$(grep -Eo "gulp.task\((['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"],)" gulpfile.js 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo "['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"]" | sed s/"['\"]"//g | sort)"

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
Use local gradlew

Instead of requiring to add `cwd` to path, just use `gradlew` from the local folder. This is how most gradle wrapper projects are laid out

svn plugin: urldecode repo info

The repo location reported by `svn` is a URL. If you don't decode it before using it a prompt, the `%` sequences will be misinterpreted by prompt expansion and give screwy results.
Fixes #2233
removed_lines: -    root=`svn info 2> /dev/null | sed -n 's/^Working Copy Root Path: //p'`
added_lines: +    local root=`svn info 2> /dev/null | sed -n 's/^Working Copy Root Path: //p'`

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: 'ip' command compatibility and much quicker external IP detection
removed_lines: -    ifconfig  | grep 'inet addr:'| grep -v '127.0.0.1' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}'
added_lines: +        ifconfig  | grep 'inet addr:'| grep -v '127.0.0.1' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: [wd] v0.3.1: Improved completion and bug fixes
use ls instead of find to avoid incompatibility with gnu find
replaced hardcoded origin/{branch-name} with @{upstream} which gets the upstream branch since git 1.7.0
removed_lines: -  if $(echo "$(command git log origin/$(current_branch)..HEAD 2> /dev/null)" | grep '^commit' &> /dev/null); then

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: added the linux implementation to the battery plugin

`fc -l 1` instead of `history` in zsh_stats #2501

$HIST_STAMP breaks zsh_stats. see #2501
removed_lines: -  history | awk '{CMD[$2]++;count++;}END { for (a in CMD)print CMD[a] " " CMD[a]/count*100 "% " a;}' | grep -v "./" | column -c3 -s " " -t | sort -nr | nl |  head -n20
added_lines: +  fc -l 1 | awk '{CMD[$2]++;count++;}END { for (a in CMD)print CMD[a] " " CMD[a]/count*100 "% " a;}' | grep -v "./" | column -c3 -s " " -t | sort -nr | nl |  head -n20

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: escape dashes
removed_lines: -alias gunwip='git log -n 1 | grep -q -c "--wip--" && git reset HEAD~1'
added_lines: +alias gunwip='git log -n 1 | grep -q -c "\-\-wip\-\-" && git reset HEAD~1'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
Fix issues with special characters when running mvn

Setting the locale to C will stabilize sed, so it won't stop processing
the mvn output when it encounters invalid characters (like binary data)

This makes it also more viable to add the `alias mvn='mvn-color`, as
the coloring is less obtrusive, and there won't be any issues with sed
breaking because of an invalid character inside the stream

gitfast: update to upstream v1.8.4

Signed-off-by: Felipe Contreras <felipe.contreras@gmail.com>
Fix bad ps syntax in ssh-agent plugin

Add more capable hg incoming and outgoing count handling

The original hgic and hgoc aliases worked well for the default path, but attempting to call them with a different path failed. I created functions to handle them instead.
Fix: "\s" is a gawk-specific regexp operator.
Fix symfony command completion 'permission denied'
removed_lines: -    ./symfony | sed "1,/Available tasks/d" | awk 'BEGIN { cat=null; } /^[A-Za-z]+$/ { cat = $1; } /^  :[a-z]+/ { print cat $1; }'
added_lines: +    php symfony | sed "1,/Available tasks/d" | awk 'BEGIN { cat=null; } /^[A-Za-z]+$/ { cat = $1; } /^  :[a-z]+/ { print cat $1; }'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
gitfast: synchronize with upstream

Up to version 1.8.2.1.

Signed-off-by: Felipe Contreras <felipe.contreras@gmail.com>

This fixes checking for git untracked items

The grep expression was incorrect for normal grep. Adding
the `-E` option fixed the escaping of the question marks.
fix git_prompt_status() to not say the repository has untracked files all the time

Fix Symfony2 command completion 'permission denied'

app/console by default (if you create a new Symfony project via composer create-project or by downloading it from symfony.com) is not executable. Therefore I get the following error:

sf2 _symfony2_get_command_list:1: permission denied: app/console 
_symfony2_get_command_list:1: permission denied: app/console
_symfony2_get_command_list:1: permission denied: app/console

To make command completion work without changing app/console you just have to let php run it.
added_lines: +	php app/console --no-ansi | sed "1,/Available commands/d" | awk '/^  [a-z]+/ { print $1 }'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
Don't drop everything after a trailing slash, as this breaks standard svn branches:
^/branches/featurename
^/releases/Release-vx.y
^/trunk
OpenBSD doesn't have -ef flags for ps. Both linux and OpenBSD have -x flags which works just as greate here

Jake-node plugin : update
- remove the need to write a `jake_tasks` in the directory
- use most recent usage of completion with zsh
- tested for MacOSX and Ubuntu
Better cake completion: don't barf on options, and don't clobber user's namespace
Minor corrections to deletion detection in git_prompt_status

Adds support for $(current_branch) on git_parse_ahead()

Changes method git_parse_ahead() on git.zsh lib file. Before this
change it checked directly against origin/master branch. Now it
uses $(current_branch) to check for not pushed changes against
the current remote branch.

Escape &'s in path name.  Need to find general function for escaping all
shell metacharacters.
